topics: C 4.1 – Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic or heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five kingdoms
C 4.2 – Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C 4.3 – Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
C 4.4 – Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level (using models and/or simulations)

C 4.5 – Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection in terms of changes — in allele frequency in population, structure, and function of organisms

units.unit_name: Linkages between Scientific Knowledge and Other Curricular Areas
topics: C 5.1 – Explores how literature and arts have influenced Science

C 5.2 – Examines a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics (for example, Marie Curie, Jenner, treatment of patients with mental illnesses, the story of the atomic bomb, green revolution and GMOs, conservation of biodiversity)
C 5.3 – Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects (sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music; use of muscles in dance form and sports)

Acids and Bases – definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH- ions, identification using indicators, chemical properties, examples and uses, neutralization, concept of pH scale (Definition relating to logarithm not required), importance of pH in everyday life; preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching Powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris
Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds; Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention

Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon, Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature of alkanes, alkenes, alkyne and carbon compounds containing functional groups (halogens, alcohol, ketones, aldehydes). Chemical properties of carbon compounds (combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reaction). Ethanol and Ethanoic acid (only properties and uses), soaps and detergents

topics: Chemical Reactions and Equations
Studying the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I) passing through it and determine its resistance. Also plotting a graph between V and I.

practicals.title: Finding pH of various samples
practicals.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
practicals.details: Find the pH of dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution, Dilute Ethanoic Acid solution, Lemon juice, Water, and Dilute Hydrogen Carbonate solution using pH paper/universal indicator.

practicals.title: Studying dependence of potential difference on current
practicals.details: Study the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I) passing through it, determine its resistance, and plot a graph between V and I.

practicals.title: Determination of equivalent resistance
practicals.details: Determine the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel.

practicals.title: Preparing temporary mount of leaf peel
practicals.details: Prepare a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.

practicals.title: Showing CO2 release during respiration
practicals.details: Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.

practicals.title: Studying properties of acetic acid
practicals.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
practicals.details: Study the odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, and reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate of acetic acid (ethanoic acid).

practicals.title: Determination of focal length
practicals.details: Determine the focal length of a concave mirror and a convex lens by obtaining the image of a distant object.

practicals.title: Studying binary fission and budding
practicals.details: Study binary fission in Amoeba and budding in yeast and Hydra with the help of prepared slides.

practicals.title: Identification of embryo parts
practicals.details: Identify the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed (pea, gram, or red kidney bean).

activities.title: Studying the properties of acids and bases on reaction with indicators and metals
activities.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
activities.chapter_name: Chemical Reactions and Equations / Acids, Bases and Salts
activities.details: Observe reactions of HCl and NaOH with litmus solution (blue/red), zinc metal, and solid sodium carbonate to identify properties.

activities.title: Classifying chemical reactions through observation
activities.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
activities.details: Perform and observe combination, decomposition, displacement, and double displacement reactions including action of water on quicklime and heat on ferrous sulphate crystals.

activities.title: Determining reactivity series of metals
activities.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
activities.details: Observe the action of Zn, Fe, Cu, and Al on salt solutions (ZnSO4, FeSO4, CuSO4, Al2(SO4)3) to arrange metals in decreasing order of reactivity.

activities.title: Comparative cleaning capacity of soap
activities.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
activities.details: Study the cleaning capacity of a soap sample in soft water versus hard water.

activities.title: Tracing light path through a glass slab
activities.details: Trace the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence and measure angles of incidence, refraction, and emergence.

activities.title: Tracing light path through a prism
activities.details: Trace the path of rays of light passing through a glass prism to observe refraction and dispersion.

activities.title: Portfolio write-up on non-assessed topics
activities.unit_name: Unit I, Unit II, Unit IV
activities.chapter_name: Periodic Classification of Elements / Heredity and Evolution / Electric Effects
activities.details: Prepare a brief write-up in the portfolio for topics included formatively (Periodic Classification, Heredity/Evolution, Electric Effects) to reinforce understanding without summative assessment.

projects.title: Scientific Inquiry Plan Design
projects.unit_name: Unit VIII: Nature of Science by Doing Science
projects.chapter_name: Nature of Science by Doing Science
projects.details: Design and implement a plan for scientific inquiry involving hypothesis formulation, variable identification, accurate instrument use, data representation in multiple modes, and communication of findings using scientific terminology.

projects.title: Case Study on Science in Human Life
projects.unit_name: Unit V: Linkages between Scientific Knowledge and Other Curricular Areas
projects.chapter_name: Linkages between Scientific Knowledge and Other Curricular Areas
projects.details: Examine a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics (e.g., Marie Curie, Jenner, green revolution, GMOs, conservation of biodiversity).

marks_distribution: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour (25 Marks)
Unit II: World of Living (25 Marks)
Unit IV: Effects of Current (13 Marks)
question_paper_design: Competency: Formulate, Analyze, Evaluate and Create (20%)
Typology of Questions: VSA (including objective type), Assertion-Reasoning, SA, LA, Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions
bloom_levels: State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize (Knowledge)
Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish (Application)
Interpret, analyze, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct (Analysis/Evaluation/Creation)
practical_assessment: List of Experiments: 14 specific experiments covering pH determination, acid-base properties, reaction classification, reactivity series, Ohm's law verification, resistance determination, stomata observation, CO2 respiration, acetic acid properties, soap cleaning capacity, focal length determination, light refraction through prism, binary fission/budding observation, and embryo identification
Assessment of Practical Skills in Science (CBSE Publication)
project_assessment: Portfolio: Brief write-up on topics not assessed in year-end examination (Periodic Classification of Elements; Heredity and Evolution; Electric Effects of Electric Current)
Credit given for Periodic Assessment/Portfolio based on joyful and experiential opportunities
